data.title: InPlay Oil Up Nearly 12% As Q1 Result Overshadowed By Updated 2025 Capital Budget Post Closing of "Highly Accretive" Pembina Asset Buy
data.description:
InPlay Oil's shares were up near 12% at last look Thursday on the TSX as the company reported first-quarter results that appear to have been overshadowed by the updating of its 2025 capital budget post closing of the Pembina asset acquisition, that is already providing "exceptional results from the first quarter drilling program".

Following closing of the "highly accretive" acquisition on April 7, 2025, InPlay said it had provided initial pro forma guidance inclusive of the assets. It added guidance "reflects the exceptional operational performance across the company's expanded asset base, while taking into account the current volatile commodity price environment. It also underscores InPlay's continued commitment to maximizing free cash flow to support ongoing debt reduction, while positioning the company to support its return to shareholder strategy."

The company booked a comprehensive loss of $2.9 million, or a loss of $0.19 basic and $0.18 diluted per share, compared to a comprehensive income of near $1.7 million, or $0.11 per basic and diluted share a year earlier. There was an analyst consensus forecast of $0.17 per share compiled by FactSet.

Meanwhile, the board approved an updated capital program of $53 million to $60 million for 2025, and InPlay plans to drill about 5.5 to 7.5 net extended reach horizontal cardium wells over the rest of the year.

InPlay said first quarter results "exceeded expectations, driven in part by the outperformance of newly drilled wells" at Pembina Cardium Unit #7. It noted a two well pad delivered average initial production rates of 677 boe/d (75% light oil and NGLs) over the first 30 days and 492 boe/d (66% light oil and NGLs) over the first 60 days, both significantly above expectations. "Over the initial two-month period, production from these wells was more than 100% above our type curve. These wells ranked in the top-ten for production rates for all Cardium wells in the basin for the month of March."

Complementing InPlay's "strong operational momentum", Obsidian drilled four (4.0 net) wells on the acquired assets in the first quarter. The first two (2.0 net) wells, which started production mid quarter, are outperforming internal type curve by approximately 50% with average IP rates of 304 boe/d (91% light oil and NGLs) over the first 30 days and 295 boe/d (85% light oil and NGLs) over the first 60 days. The remaining two wells, brought online in the final days of the first quarter, are performing more than 350% above our internal type curve, with average IP rates per well of 887 boe/d (88% light oil and NGLs) over their initial 30 day period.

"The company is very excited about the highly accretive Pembina Acquisition announced February 19, 2025 and had anticipated strong results from the combined assets. The exceptional results from the first quarter drilling program, combined with the outperformance of base production, have driven current field estimated production to approximately 21,500 boe/d (64% light oil and NGLs) significantly exceeding what we had initially forecasted at the announcement of the acquisition. Given the current volatility in commodity prices, this material outperformance provides the company with significant flexibility to scale back our capital program, providing "more for less" while maintaining our production forecasts, allowing for more aggressive debt repayment even in a lower pricing environment."

data.title: Costco Wholesale Sees April Sales Growth With Increase in Discretionary Spending, UBS Says
data.description:
Costco Wholesale's core comparable sales saw steady growth momentum in April, driven by continued market share gains, particularly in discretionary categories, UBS Securities said in a note emailed Thursday.

Non-food categories posted mid-to-high single-digit growth, driven by areas such as jewelry, major appliances, and home furnishings, according to the note.

"The comp was yet again largely driven by traffic, which was up 4.2% on a worldwide basis and 5% in the US," the firm said. This steady footfall should support sales and enable better expense leverage, helping Costco maintain strong margins.

Improvements in digital capabilities, such as faster delivery, expanded stock-keeping units and mobile app enhancements, resulted in a 13% year-over-year growth in e-commerce sales in April, UBS added.

The firm has a buy rating on Costco's stock with a $1,205 price target.

data.description:
RNS Number : 9502H Croma Security Solutions Group PLC 08 May 2025  

8 May 2025

Croma Security Solutions Group Plc

("CSSG", "Croma", "the "Company" or the "Group")

Director/PCA Dealings

Croma Security Solutions Group plc (AIM:CSSG)  announces that the Company has been advised that Roberto Fiorentino, CEO, has transferred ordinary shares of 5p each in the capital of the Company held by him in his dealing account to his and his wife's ISAs.

On 8 May 2025, Mr Fiorentino  sold 60,130 Ordinary Shares at 82p per Ordinary Share from his dealing account and subsequently purchased 30,065 Ordinary Shares at 82p per Ordinary Share into his ISA. In addition on 8 May 2025, Mrs Fiorentino purchased 30,065 Ordinary Shares at 82p per Ordinary Share into her ISA.

Following these transactions Mr Fiorentino and his wife's total holdings remain unchanged. Mr Fiorentino's direct and indirect holding in the Company is 3,762,029*, which represents 27.3 per cent of the share capital of the Company.

* Includes ordinary shares held by Natalie Fiorentino (Roberto Fiorentino's wife) and by related trusts and Sofia Fiorentino.

Further detail is provided below in accordance with the EU Market Abuse Regulation.

data.title: Hims & Hers Health Plans $450 Million Convertible Notes Offering
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health said Thursday it plans to offer $450 million of convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to institutional investors.

The company said it would grant the initial buyers an option to purchase up to an additional $67.5 million of the notes.

The company said it plans to use the proceeds for general corporate purposes, including accelerating global expansion.

data.title: CITY HOLDING CO Directors Increase Holdings with Recent Purchases
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
File W H IiiDirectorPurchase$16,807Form 4
Fisher Robert DDirectorPurchase$19,025Form 4
Reyes Javier ADirectorPurchase$13,539Form 4
Strong-Treister Diane WDirectorPurchase$14,006Form 4

Several directors at CITY HOLDING CO have recently increased their stakes in the company through the purchase of shares, as reported in SEC Form 4 filings.

File W H III, a director at CITY HOLDING CO, purchased 144 shares of Common Stock on May 7, 2025, at a price of $116.72 per share, for a total of $16,807. Following this transaction, File W H III now directly owns 26,943 shares and indirectly owns 999 shares for his son, W H File IV.

Robert D Fisher, another director at the company, acquired 163 shares at the same price of $116.72 per share, totaling $19,025. This purchase brings Fisher's direct ownership to 45,508 shares, which includes 439.93231 shares acquired through the dividend reinvestment plan.

Javier A Reyes, also a director, purchased 116 shares at $116.72 each, totaling $13,539. Reyes's direct ownership in the company has increased to 1,617 shares following this transaction.

Diane W Strong-Treister, serving as a director for CITY HOLDING CO, bought 120 shares at $116.72 per share, totaling $14,006. Post-transaction, Strong-Treister directly owns 3,247 shares and indirectly owns 1,900 shares through the Diane W. Strong Treister Revocable Trust.

data.title: Greek lender Eurobank posts 9.4% rise in first-quarter profit
data.description:
Greek lender Eurobank reported a 9.4% increase in first-quarter profit on Thursday, driven by higher net fee and commission income.

The bank's reported net earnings for the January-March period were 314 million euros ($354.66 million), up from 287 million euros in the same period last year.

($1 = 0.8853 euros)

data.title: Trump to Slash Tariffs on UK-Made Cars to 10%, The Telegraph Reports
Fair Isaac Plans $1.5 Billion Senior Notes Offering
data.description:
Fair Isaac said Thursday that it plans to offer $1.5 billion in aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2033 in a private offering.

The data analytics company said it intends to use the offering proceeds to repay certain indebtedness outstanding under its existing unsecured revolving credit facility and unsecured term loans, and for general corporate purposes.

The offering remains subject to market and certain other conditions, the company said.

data.title: Saga Pure ASA - Mandatory notification of trades - Acceptances under Mandatory Offer
data.description:
Saga Pure ASA

Reference is made to the stock exchange notice dated 11 April 2025 regarding the commencement of the acceptance period for the mandatory offer by Tycoon Industrier AS ("Tycoon" or the "Offeror") for shares in Saga Pure ASA ("Saga Pure" or the "Company") not owned by the Offeror, at an offer price of NOK 1.33 per share in Saga Pure (the "Offer Price") (the "Mandatory Offer").

The Offeror is a close associate of the Company's Board Member Øystein Stray Spetalen and is therefore required to disclose acceptances received under the Mandatory Offer on an ongoing basis.

El Investment AS, a company controlled by CEO in Saga Pure Espen Lundaas, have accepted the mandatory offer for all the 2,300,000 shares owned by El Investment AS.

Tore Jakob Berg, CFO in Saga Pure, have accepted the mandatory offer for all his 566,979 shares.

Please see the attached notifications of trading for information on acceptances received by the Offeror on 8 May 2025.

data.title: Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corp reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.66​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.80. The mean expectation of nine analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.49 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.25 to $1.90 per share.

  • Revenue rose 0.4% to $1.20 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.21 billion.

  • Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.46​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $56 million.

  • Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corp shares had fallen by 9.4% this quarter and lost 35.2% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 23.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, five analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 6 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the hotels, motels & cruise lines peer group is "hold."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corp is $97.00

data.title: Kohl's Brings in New Chair After CEO's Firing
data.description:
By Dean Seal

Kohl's tapped the former boss of Northwestern Mutual to be its independent chair after its previous chairman was named interim chief executive in the wake of Ashley Buchanan's firing.

The retailer said Thursday that Michael Bender will no longer serve as chair and that John Schlifske, who retired as Northwestern Mutual's CEO at the end of last year, has been appointed independent chair of the board.

The board has also formed a committee to search for the company's next chief executive after firing Buchanan last week. The Wall Street Journal reported that Buchanan had tried to funnel business to a romantic partner, Chandra Holt, with whom he currently lives.

Kohl's appointed Bender to be its interim CEO following the ouster, making him the fourth CEO in three years for the struggling department-store chain.

The company also said Thursday that Christine Day has resigned from the board. The decision wasn't due to any disagreements with the company or any matter relating to its operations, policies or practices, according to a securities filing.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com
